component.name: Feather Shot
component.summary: Wayland-first screenshot tool with annotation and scrolling capture
Wayland専用スクリーンショット(注釈・スクロールキャプチャ対応)

description.p: Flameshot-style screenshot tool built Wayland-first. Freeze the screen, drag a selection, and annotate it in place with a floating toolbar — pen, line, arrow, rectangle, ellipse, highlighter, text, blur, pixelate, auto-numbered markers and crop. Save to disk or copy to the clipboard.
It also records a scrolling capture while you scroll and stitches the frames into one tall image.

100% local: no upload button, no accounts, no telemetry, and no network code at all. Every capture goes through xdg-desktop-portal, so it works on GNOME, KDE Plasma, Hyprland, Sway and anything with a portal backend.
Follow-up release for the updater remove command that keeps the GitHub release workflow green when optional AUR publishing cannot authenticate.
Adds a GTK-free updater remove command for uninstalling files managed by install.sh while keeping user configuration, and extends the release tooling with optional AUR publishing support.

Makes optional RemoteDesktop-portal auto-scroll discoverable from the scrolling-capture window, gates it on actual portal availability, adds diagnostics and tests for the stop policy, and documents per-desktop behavior while keeping manual scrolling the default.

Makes the region-overlay annotation toolbar theme-independent so the tool buttons stay high-contrast and readable — previously their labels rendered white-on-white and the buttons collapsed into ovals under dark or third-party GTK themes — and restores the blue active-tool highlight.
Improves light/dark theme handling by syncing GTK with the desktop portal appearance setting, strengthening custom overlay contrast and reducing the region-selection dim layer for better readability.
Completes release packaging: automated AppImage, wheel, sdist, AUR bundle and checksum generation, plus corrected AUR version metadata.

Adds an open-save-folder action to the screenshot UI, fixes source launches when PATH points to a non-distro Python without GTK bindings, and cleans up GI version warnings found during a real GNOME Wayland validation pass.
Release hygiene: default shortcut wording, settings test file-handle cleanup, and refreshed headless validation.
Bug fixes: auto-scroll fallback message, and a capture-window close-button hang; added CI.
Editor and capture goodies: step-arrow/speech-bubble/emoji annotations, colour presets, WebP/AVIF export, local OCR/QR, a recent-screenshots gallery, a settings window, and GIF recording.
First cuts of multi-monitor edge snapping, fractional-scaling hairline alignment, and optional RemoteDesktop-portal auto-scroll.
Scrolling capture now works without GStreamer via a manual repeated-screenshot fallback that feeds the same stitcher.
Reliable Ctrl+PrtSc global shortcut with desktop-aware setup and diagnostics, a window-capture mode via the portal picker, and a gettext localization backend for adding languages.

Editor gains a select tool (move/delete/restyle shapes), multi-line text with outline, flatten-and-blur, and pin-to-screen. Scriptable capture flags, faster and more robust scroll stitching, a bundled clipboard holder, and packaging.
Unified app: added edit and diagnose modes and reverse-DNS app id.
